articles.title: Oil Climbs as US-Iran Deadlock Lifts Bond Yields
articles.description:
Hardika Singh, Fundstrat Economic Strategist says that tech stock earnings are really driving the stock market more than the war in Iran. 
A renewed advance in oil prices sent bonds lower after the US and Iran failed to agree on terms to end their war, dashing hopes for a revival of the Strait of Hormuz while stoking inflation concerns. The Treasury market, which has priced out the odds of Federal Reserve rate cuts this year amid the Middle East conflict, saw an increase in yields. Another rally in chipmakers left the S&P 500 at all-time highs, but most of the US equity benchmark’s shares retreated (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Palantir’s true believers are wearing this jacket
articles.description:
In late April, Palantir - the software company that, in recent years, has perhaps become best known for its defense industry contracts and work with Immigration and Customs Enforcement - announced that it would be adding new products to its merch store. The latest offering was a cotton chore coat. 
At $239 and in bright blue and black options, the jacket looks like a standard offering that has, by way of photographer Bill Cunningham, trickled down into mainstream menswear for years. This jacket is a pastiche of 19th century French workwear that was worn by people actually doing physical labor; the only noticeable difference is that a dainty …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Drug gang attacks ‘force hundreds of Indigenous families to flee’ in Mexico
articles.description:
Violence in Guerrero state has driven as many as 1,000 households from their homes, rights group says
Hundreds of Indigenous families have been forced to flee their homes in the mountains of central Mexico by intense attacks from a local criminal group, including drone bombings, an Indigenous rights organisation said on Monday.
A gang known as Los Ardillos has been carrying out attacks in Guerrero state for years, but they started to intensify last week. Villages were subjected to eight hours of bombings on Saturday, the National Indigenous Congress said, forcing between 800 to 1,000 families to flee to other towns.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Apple brings encrypted RCS chats to iPhone
articles.description:
Apple now lets you have encrypted RCS conversations with Android users through the Messages app on iOS. As part of iOS 26.5, which was released on Monday, Apple added support for end-to-end encrypted RCS messaging in beta, meaning that Apple and Google can't see your messages while they're sent. 
With iOS 26.5, you'll see a lock icon and a small "Encrypted" message at the top of the chat to note when you're having an encrypted conversation with an Android user and are on a carrier that supports the feature. Apple says that encryption will be on by default and "will be automatically enabled over time for new and existing RCS conversations."  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Testing for ‘Bad Cholesterol’ Doesn’t Tell the Whole Story
articles.description: There’s a more accurate way of measuring who’s at risk for cholesterol-related health issues. So why don’t more doctors use it?

articles.title: iOS end-to-end encrypted RCS messaging begins rolling today in beta
articles.description: Look for the new lock icon in your Android chats.

articles.title: Google announces its first-ever discovery of a zero-day exploit made with AI
articles.description: The Google Threat Intelligence Group said its proactive measures stopped a "mass exploitation event."

articles.title: The FCC Received Hundreds of Complaints About Bad Bunny’s ‘Vulgar’ Super Bowl Performance
articles.description: The complaints, obtained by WIRED, described Bad Bunny’s performance as being overly sexual and protested that the show was in Spanish.

articles.title: OpenADR and Matter are collaborating to let your smart home talk to the grid
articles.description: This should make it easier to connect appliances to demand response programs.

articles.title: FCC commissioner joins Disney's free-speech fight
articles.description: Walt Disney Co. has picked up a vocal ally in its fight against the Federal Communications Commission: one of the panel's three commissioners.

articles.title: Three things in AI to watch, according to a Nobel-winning economist
articles.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. A few months before he was awarded the Nobel Prize in economics in 2024, Daron Acemoglu published a paper that earned him few fans in Silicon Valley. Contrary to what Big Tech…

topStories.title: There's an Unhinged New Video Game About Trump and the Iran War
topStories.description: The game, developed by the group of anonymous artists known as Secret Handshake, is available online and in person in Washington, DC.

topStories.title: OpenAI sued by spouse of FSU shooting victim
topStories.description: Florida's attorney general also opened an investigation into ChatGPT recently on similar grounds.

topStories.title: Michael Burry is not a believer: 'For any stocks going parabolic reduce positions almost entirely'
topStories.description: Michael Burry said investors should "reject greed" as enthusiasm around artificial intelligence and momentum-driven trades pushes valuations sharply higher.

topStories.title: Fears of an AI breakthrough force the U.S. and China to talk
topStories.description: A Trump administration once eager to gun for technological supremacy is now, for the first time, reckoning with the power AI could unleash if left unchecked.

topStories.title: Taiwan fears Trump will speak off-script on its fate in Beijing
topStories.description: Taiwanese officials fear even the most subtle rhetorical change in policy from Trump could imperil a delicate status quo that has held for decades.

topStories.title: Big donors backed Harris in 2024. For 2028, they're not so sure
topStories.description: Though few in the party want to criticize Harris, few appear ready to endorse her. Conversations about her prospects often come down to one thing: Democrats' anxiety about winning.

topStories.title: Cartel wars, El Chapo's sons, a mysterious kidnapping and an extraordinary U.S. indictment
topStories.description: The U.S. case against a Mexican governor is entwined with the mysterious kidnapping of Sinaloa cartel kingpin Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada. That betrayal may have triggered a political and criminal reckoning on both sides of the border.

topStories.title: BTS came to Mexico and these fans will never be the same
topStories.description: More than 1. million Mexicans tried to get tickets to this week's BTS shows in Mexico City. Many of those who failed showed up anyway.

topStories.title: Palestinians Stream Back to Northern Gaza on Foot
topStories.description: Israel allowed displaced Gazans to begin crossing a military zone that bisects the enclave after a deadlock over hostage releases was broken.

topStories.title: Leading China Property Developer Reports Huge loss, in Sign of Widening Real-Estate Woes
topStories.description: Troubles at Vanke raise questions about the continued spread of the property crisis and whether the Chinese state will step in.

dailyNews.title: Freed Israeli Hostages Still Had Shrapnel in Their Bodies From Oct. 7 Attack
dailyNews.description: Some of the women were held alone for extended periods and spent eight months in tunnels, an Israeli medical official said.

dailyNews.title: Suspected Sabotage of Deep-Sea Cable Triggers First NATO-Led Response
dailyNews.description: The alliance mounted its first coordinated response to a suspected sabotage campaign against critical infrastructure after another cable was severed in the Baltic Sea.

dailyNews.title: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Enter Congo's Safe-Haven City
dailyNews.description: Residents of Goma reported gunfire and shelling after rebels overran Congolese troops. U.N. officials estimated that more than one million displaced people were now inside the city.

dailyNews.title: Cocaine-Funded Gangs Shake Colombia Years After Peace Pact
dailyNews.description: Attacks by powerful militias against civilians reflect the state’s inaction eight years after a peace accord removed a powerful rebel group from the field.

dailyNews.title: Brian Hooker's boat seized by Coast Guard after wife goes overboard in Bahamas
dailyNews.description: The U.S. Coast Guard has seized the boat used by Americans Brian and Lynette Hooker.

dailyNews.title: Elon Musk and other CEOs among Trump's delegation heading to China
dailyNews.description: Elon Musk, Tim Cook and other high-powered business leaders will be a part of the U.S. delegation traveling to China this week.

dailyNews.title: Oil Climbs as US-Iran Deadlock Lifts Bond Yields
dailyNews.description:
Hardika Singh, Fundstrat Economic Strategist says that tech stock earnings are really driving the stock market more than the war in Iran. 
A renewed advance in oil prices sent bonds lower after the US and Iran failed to agree on terms to end their war, dashing hopes for a revival of the Strait of Hormuz while stoking inflation concerns. The Treasury market, which has priced out the odds of Federal Reserve rate cuts this year amid the Middle East conflict, saw an increase in yields. Another rally in chipmakers left the S&P 500 at all-time highs, but most of the US equity benchmark’s shares retreated (Source: Bloomberg)

dailyNews.title: Victor Wembanyama avoids suspension for elbow on Naz Reid, will play in pivotal Game 5
dailyNews.description: Victor Wembanyama will not be suspended for his elbow on Naz Reid in Game 4, the NBA decided, clearing him to play in the Spurs' Game 5 on Tuesday.

dailyNews.title: 6 found dead in shipping container at rail yard near Texas border
dailyNews.description: Police confirmed that there were six people dead, five men and one woman.

dailyNews.title: Russia and Ukraine accuse each other of violating 72-hour ceasefire
dailyNews.description: The U.S.-brokered 72-hour ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine expires Monday as both countries accuse each other of violating it. Michael Bociurkiw, a senior fellow at the Atlantic Council, joins with more.

dailyNews.title: Andes virus linked to cruise ship deaths raises concern over human-to-human spread
dailyNews.description: The Andes virus strain linked to the MV Hondius cruise ship outbreak is the only hantavirus capable of spreading person to person, the WHO says.

dailyNews.title: Trump plans suspension of federal gasoline tax amid surging fuel prices after Iran war
dailyNews.description: Fuel prices across the United States have risen sharply since the conflict involving the US, Israel and Iran began on February 28. According to AAA, the average national gasoline price reached $4.52 per gallon on Monday — roughly 50% higher than the average price before the conflict began.

topStories.title: Trump seeks to pause federal gas tax as prices soar amid Iran conflict
topStories.description: Congress would have to approve the proposal, and passing the savings on to drivers, rather than oil companies, would be difficult to enforce.

topStories.title: Elon Musk and Tim Cook among CEOs expected to accompany Trump on China trip
topStories.description: A total of 17 US executives are set to join the president on his visit, where he will meet his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping.

topStories.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
topStories.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.

topStories.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire is on 'massive life support'
topStories.description: The US president criticises an Iranian counteroffer to end the war, and says the month-long ceasefire is "unbelievably weak".

topStories.title: Kennedy Is Driving a Vast Inquiry Into Vaccines, Despite His Public Silence
topStories.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has toned down his public criticism of vaccines, under orders from the White House. But inside his department, a sprawling research effort is a top priority.

topStories.title: Trump Proposes Suspending Federal Gas Tax Until Prices Fall
topStories.description: Congress would have to agree to any suspension, and dropping federal taxes would not make up for the steep increase in prices since the war began.

topStories.title: STAT+: Trump pivots on kratom derivative 7-OH, floating approval for some forms
topStories.description: Last year, the FDA had warned that natural opioids in kratom were being turned into harmful products. Now President Trump says approval should be considered.

dailyNews.title: Trump after rejecting Iran’s proposal, says ceasefire at its ‘weakest’ point and on ‘massive life support’
dailyNews.description: Speaking at the Oval Office, Trump sharply criticized Iran’s response to ongoing negotiations, calling the proposal a “piece of garbage.” Trump doubled down on his criticism and said he did not even finish reading the document.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Will Discuss US Arms Sales To Taiwan With Xi During China Trip
dailyNews.description: The United States recognises only Beijing but under domestic law is required to provide weapons to Taiwan so that it can defend itself.

dailyNews.title: Trump says ceasefire is on ‘life support’ after rejecting Iran’s proposal
dailyNews.description: US President Donald Trump said the ceasefire with Iran was “on life support” after rejecting Tehran’s latest proposal.

dailyNews.title: Trump Plans US Gas Tax Cut As Iran War Spurs Price Surge
dailyNews.description: US federal taxes on gasoline amount to 18.4 cents per gallon on gasoline and 24.4 cents per gallon for diesel, according to the US Energy Information Administration (EIA).

dailyNews.title: In Trump administration battle over AI, U.S. spy agencies seek more power
dailyNews.description: As the White House grapples with cybersecurity threats from artificial intelligence models, intelligence officials want sway in AI policy overseen by Commerce.

dailyNews.title: Iran vows to fight on as Trump says ceasefire is "on life support"
dailyNews.description: President Trump is expected to encourage China to pressure Iran into making a deal to end the war when he visits Beijing later this week and meets with President Xi Jinping.

dailyNews.title: Soros-backed prosecutor hit with rebuke after dismissing probe as Trump ‘partisan attacks’: ‘Hey scumbag’
dailyNews.description: Soros-backed prosecutor Steve Descano dismisses a DOJ probe into alleged sweetheart deals for illegal aliens as a partisan attack by Trump's team.

dailyNews.title: China’s crop-control chokehold puts America’s dinner table in danger
dailyNews.description: A seventh-generation Indiana farmer explains why Trump's executive order securing domestic glyphosate production is vital to America's food security.

dailyNews.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire agreement is on "massive life support"
dailyNews.description: President Trump told reporters in the Oval Office on Monday the U.S.-Iran ceasefire agreement is on "massive life support." CBS News' Nancy Cordes has more.

dailyNews.title: Trump proposes gas tax holiday, but savings may be limited
dailyNews.description: Suspending the federal gas tax would have a modest impact on fuel prices, while also requiring congressional approval.

dailyNews.title: Lawyers for correspondents' dinner shooting suspect seek to disqualify Pirro, Blanche from case
dailyNews.description: Cole Allen, the suspected White House Correspondents' Dinner shooter, pleaded not guilty to all charges on Monday. Allen's lawyers are seeking to disqualify all U.S. attorneys in the Washington, D.C., office from the case, including the District of Columbia's Jeanine Pirro and acting Attorney General Todd Blanche. CBS News' Nicole Sganga has more.

dailyNews.title: Trump approves plan to fire FDA Commissioner Marty Makary
dailyNews.description: Marty Makary has served as Food and Drug Administration commissioner since March 2025.
